definition:
1. of  or belonging to war , (Pindar), etc.: —; τὰ πολέμια whatever belongs to war, war and its business , (Herdotus Historicus), etc. 
2. of  or like an enemy, hostile , (Pindar), etc.:—;as Substantive an enemy , (Herdotus Historicus), attic; οἱ π. the enemy , (Thucydides): —; τὸ π. hostility , (Thucydides) 
3. generally, opposed, adverse , (Herdotus Historicus) 
4. of  or from the enemy , (Aeschulus Tragicus); πολέμια, ων, τά, enemy's wares, contraband , (Aristophanes Comicus): —; ἡ πολεμία (i.e. γῆ, χώρα); the enemy's country , (Xenophon Historicus) 
5. adverb -ίως, in hostile manner , (Thucydides) (ML)
